title: 'If they catch me, it's over for me': Senegal's LGBTQ+ community lives in daily fear of repression
subhead: In March, Senegal passed a law increasing penalties for same-sex relations, leaving LGBTQ+ people living in fear. Several described their deeply uncertain daily lives, gripped by the constant anxiety of being arrested.

title: 'We were meant to take as many jewels as we could': The confessions of two Louvre burglars
'It would have been enough to learn the terrible lessons from Gaza to anticipate the current impasse over Iran'
Alfred Dreyfus statue finally finds place in heart of Paris
subhead: The bronze sculpture created by the artist Tim in 1985 was installed in front of the Cour de Cassation, the court that eventually cleared Dreyfus's name.

title: 'I would do a line before service, hidden in the bathroom': How cocaine is flooding the restaurant industry
subhead: Cocaine at work (2/3). From fast-food joints to Michelin-starred restaurants, drug use has become as commonplace as it is alarming in an industry driven by the pursuit of performance, a toxic party culture and grueling working conditions.

title: Sinner outlasts Zverev to win second straight Wimbledon title
subhead: 24-year-old Jannik Sinner wore down French Open champion Alexander Zverev, securing a 6-7 (7/9), 7-6 (7/2), 6-3, 6-4 win on Sunday. 'You never know how many times you are going to come back,' said the world No. 1. 'I never take it for granted.'

title: French heatwave drives up water consumption, puts supply networks under severe strain
subhead: Water usage rose by 10% to 50% in June compared to the same period in 2025, forcing already fragile infrastructure to operate at full capacity, while underground reserves and rivers weakened.

Simon Abkarian, star of 'De Gaulle': 'At school in Lebanon, my nickname was de Gaulle'
subhead: The actor and theater director plays Charles de Gaulle in the recently released two-part film. In this article, he explains how he prepared for the role of France's World War II hero.

title: Martine Barrat, a photographer without fear or limits
subhead: At 93, the artist has lost none of her vitality or curiosity. She lives at the legendary Hotel Chelsea in New York, where she remains one of the last year-round artist residents. Today, her work is the subject of a retrospective at the Rencontres d'Arles photography festival.

title: Pauline Vilain-Carlotti, geographer: 'The intensity of wildfires, which seems unprecedented, could become the new norm'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, the environmental management specialist says wildfire risks, until now mainly limited to the Mediterranean region, are likely to increase and spread across France.
